commit.message:
Implement per-frame lighting UBO for Vulkan renderer

- Replace single dummy lighting UBO with per-frame double-buffered UBOs
  (host-visible, host-coherent VMA allocations, one per frame in flight)
- Add GpuLight (64 bytes) and GpuLightingUBO (2080 bytes) structs matching
  the std140 layout in lighting.glsl
- Implement addLight(), clearLights(), setAmbientColor() with CPU-side state
- Add uploadLightingData() that memcpys lighting state to the current frame's
  mapped UBO before each draw that uses lighting
- When m_worldPass is false (UI rendering), upload ambient=(1,1,1) and
  numLights=0 so UI renders at full brightness
- Fix drawRect() to bind the lighting descriptor set at set 0 (was missing)
- Update drawSprite() and drawText() to use per-frame descriptor sets
- Per-frame descriptor sets pre-allocated from a dedicated pool

commit.message:
fix: Azure tool serialization crash causing 100% CLI fallback

tool.parameters is a voluptuous Schema, not a dict. It was being passed
directly to the JSON serializer, causing 'Object of type Schema is not
JSON serializable' on every Azure-routed request. All Azure calls failed
at ~3ms and fell back to CLI.

Fix: use voluptuous_openapi.convert() with the LLM API's custom_serializer
to properly convert tool schemas to JSON-compatible dicts, matching how
HA's built-in conversation integrations (OpenRouter, Google AI, etc.)
handle this.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(telemetry): add E2E observability — traceparent propagation, SDK OTEL, audit logging (Phase 7) (#394)

* feat(telemetry): add E2E observability — traceparent propagation, SDK OTEL, audit logging (Phase 7)

Step 7.1: W3C traceparent/tracestate propagation across queue boundary
- remote_executor: inject trace context via opentelemetry.propagate.inject
- task_runner: restore trace context around entire task execution
- copilot_session: pass TelemetryConfig to SubprocessConfig for CLI OTEL export
- pipeline: add span_attributes parameter for semantic attributes
- telemetry/tracing: add restore_trace_context() helper

Step 7.2: Structured audit logging
- gitlab_client._request(): audit.api_call with method, path, status, duration, attempts
- credential_registry.resolve_identity(): audit.auth_attempt and audit.auth_failure
- telemetry/logging: add_trace_context in foreign_pre_chain for stdlib/SDK logs

Closes #352

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(telemetry): derive HTTP endpoint for CLI OTEL export, add HTTP to console collector

The Copilot CLI uses OTLP HTTP (default port 4318), not gRPC (4317).
Previously we passed the gRPC endpoint to TelemetryConfig, which silently
failed because the CLI sent HTTP POST to a gRPC server.

- copilot_session: derive HTTP endpoint (port 4318) from gRPC endpoint;
  COPILOT_OTEL_HTTP_ENDPOINT env var overrides for custom setups
- otel_console_collector: add HTTP/protobuf listener on port 4318
  alongside existing gRPC on 4317

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(observability): add distributed tracing, audit logging, CLI OTEL sections

Update observability.md with:
- Distributed tracing across queue boundary (traceparent propagation)
- Copilot CLI OTEL integration (TelemetryConfig, HTTP endpoint derivation)
- Structured audit logging (audit.api_call, audit.auth_attempt)
- Pipeline span_attributes
- Trace context in foreign/stdlib logs
- Updated console collector script reference (actual script, not Docker example)
- Updated span hierarchy examples (local and remote executor flows)

Refactor test_tracing.py (411→300 lines):
- Parameterized restore_trace_context tests
- Parameterized QueueTaskPayload and TelemetryConfig tests
- Extracted _make_stub_pipeline() and _capture_copilot_config() helpers
- Eliminated duplicated _FakeClient class

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(infra): add OTEL HTTP→gRPC sidecar for Copilot CLI traces

The ACA managed OTEL agent only supports gRPC (port 4317) but the
Copilot CLI only supports OTLP HTTP. Add a lightweight otel-collector
sidecar to the task runner that bridges the gap:
  CLI ─HTTP→ otel-sidecar:4318 ─gRPC→ managed-agent:4317

- infra/container-apps.tf: init container writes config, sidecar
  container runs otel-collector-contrib, COPILOT_OTEL_HTTP_ENDPOINT
  env var set to localhost:4318
- helm/scaledjob.yaml: conditional sidecar when telemetry enabled
- helm/otel-sidecar-config.yaml: ConfigMap with sidecar YAML config
- docs/observability.md: document protocol mismatch and sidecar solution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(infra): use correct ACA managed agent FQDN for OTEL sidecar

The sidecar was using 'otel.service.k8se-apps:4317' but the actual
ACA managed OTEL agent hostname is 'k8se-otel.k8se-apps.svc.cluster.local:4317'.
Use env var substitution (${env:OTEL_GRPC_TARGET}) in the collector config
so the endpoint is explicit and documented.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(infra): escape OTEL env var substitution for Terraform

Terraform interprets ${} as HCL interpolation. Use $${} to produce
a literal ${env:OTEL_GRPC_TARGET} in the rendered config.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(observability): document CLI OTEL HTTP exporter limitation

CLI v1.0.24 OTLP HTTP exporter sends empty payloads — file exporter
works correctly. Sidecar infrastructure is deployed and ready for when
the CLI fixes its HTTP exporter. Also fix duplicate paragraph.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(observability): wire span_attributes, fix port derivation, add ADR-0017

Address Phase 7 review rework findings:

- Wire span_attributes to all 6 run_pipeline() callers via TaskEvent.span_attrs()
  helper (RPI-M1: pipeline spans now carry project_id, mr_iid, task_type,
  trigger_source in production)
- Fix OTLP HTTP port derivation using urlparse instead of rsplit (IV-004:
  handles portless URLs and IPv6 addresses)
- Create ADR-0017 documenting W3C traceparent propagation across queue
  boundary (RPI-m1)
- Add tests for span_attrs(), caller wiring assertions, and URL edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(observability): address minor review findings IV-002 through IV-006

- Type _detach_trace token as Token[Context] | None (IV-002)
- Add cross-reference comments between Helm and Terraform sidecar configs (IV-003)
- Remove unused _run_http_server return value (IV-005)
- Right-size ACA sidecar to 0.1 vCPU / 0.125Gi matching Helm (IV-006)

IV-007 (collector bind address) intentionally kept as 0.0.0.0 for
devcontainer host accessibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(observability): fix console collector HTTP, add trace propagation harness

Fix two bugs in otel_console_collector.py that prevented CLI spans from
being received:
- Add chunked transfer encoding support (CLI uses Transfer-Encoding:
  chunked with no Content-Length header)
- Add JSON body parsing with ignore_unknown_fields (CLI sends OTLP JSON,
  not protobuf, with newer schema fields like schemaUrl)

Also add --verbose flag for per-span detail output (trace ID, parent,
attributes) useful for verifying trace propagation.

Add scripts/test_trace_propagation.py e2e harness that creates a parent
span, runs a Copilot SDK session inside it, and verifies via the
collector that CLI spans share the same trace ID with correct parent
linkage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(observability): ensure CLI spans flush before process termination

The Copilot CLI's OTLP batch exporter fires on a 5s schedule by default.
When client.stop() terminates the CLI process immediately after session
completion, pending spans from the final LLM call are lost — this is
why CLI spans were missing in prod.

Two-part fix:
- Set OTEL_BSP_SCHEDULE_DELAY=1000 on the CLI subprocess to reduce the
  batch interval from 5s to 1s
- Add a 2s asyncio.sleep before client.stop() to guarantee at least one
  batch export cycle completes

Confirmed via e2e harness: CLI invoke_agent and chat spans now arrive
at the sidecar collector with correct trace ID and parent linkage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(infra): fix terraform fmt and ACA sidecar memory constraint

terraform fmt alignment fix for container-apps.tf, and change sidecar
memory from 0.125Gi to 0.25Gi — ACA requires ≤2 decimal places and
0.25Gi is the platform minimum.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(infra): restore ACA sidecar to 0.25/0.5Gi for valid resource tier

ACA Consumption requires total container resources to match a fixed
tier. Main container (1.0 CPU/2Gi) + sidecar must sum to 1.25/2.5Gi.
The previous 0.1/0.25Gi values produced an invalid 1.1/2.25Gi total.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(observability): replace sidecar with file-based CLI trace forwarding

The OTEL collector sidecar (init container + otel-collector-contrib image)
caused ACA job pod startup failures — the ~200MB image pull times out on
cold start with parallelism=1, so the first job pod always fails.

Replace with a simpler approach: the CLI writes spans to a per-session
JSONL file, and the app forwards them through its existing OTLP gRPC
exporter after the session completes.

New module: telemetry/cli_trace_forwarder.py
- Reads JSONL after client.stop() — file exporter writes synchronously
- Converts to ReadableSpan preserving original timestamps and trace IDs
- Exports via the app's span exporter (stored in telemetry._state)
- Failure-tolerant: logs and skips malformed lines, never fails tasks

Removed:
- infra/container-apps.tf: init_container, otel-sidecar, volume (~70 lines)
- helm/scaledjob.yaml: conditional sidecar container + volume
- helm/otel-sidecar-config.yaml: entire file
- helm/values.yaml: collector image config
- COPILOT_OTEL_HTTP_ENDPOINT env var (both platforms)
- 2s asyncio.sleep + OTEL_BSP_SCHEDULE_DELAY workarounds
- URL port derivation logic (urlparse/urlunparse)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use Next.js router for history entries and reset roomRef on disconnect

Three bugs fixed:

1. beforePopState never fired for game→lobby back navigation because
   window.history.pushState(null, ...) creates entries without __N state.
   Next.js onPopState returns early at 'if (!state)' for such entries.
   Fix: URL sync effect now uses router.push/replace (shallow) which
   creates proper __N-stamped entries. The beforePopState pushback also
   includes {__N: true, ...} state.

2. Re-joining the same room after back-navigating to welcome failed
   because roomRef.current was never cleared. handleHashChange saw
   'room !== roomRef.current' as false and skipped joinRoom.
   Fix: reset roomRef.current = '' in every disconnect/cleanup path.

3. router.asPath became stale after direct window.history calls,
   causing router.push to skip same-URL navigations.
   Fix: eliminated by using router.push/replace instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix W-Wing bridge/elim candidate ordering to match Java

In Java's getWWing, the first checkLink call uses cand2 (larger digit) as the
bridge and cand1 (smaller digit) as the elimination target. The TS port had
this reversed, trying bridge=smaller first. This caused different W-Wing
selection on puzzles where both orderings produce valid W-Wings, leading to
divergent solving paths and different scores.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Task 14: Vertex-color pipeline and primitives

Implement three vertex-color VkPipelines (triangle-list, triangle-strip,
line-list) sharing a single pipeline layout with 64-byte push constants
(mat4 projection) and vec2+vec4 vertex input matching Renderer::Vertex.

- createVertexColorPipelines(): creates all three topology pipelines using
  vertcolor.vert/frag SPIR-V shaders, alpha blending, dynamic viewport/scissor
- drawTriangleStrip(): uploads caller vertices to dynamic buffer, draws with
  triangle-strip pipeline
- drawLines(): same with line-list pipeline
- drawCircle(): generates triangle-list fill (32 segments, fan→individual
  triangles) + optional triangle-strip outline ring
- drawRoundedRect(): generates triangle-list fill from 4-corner-arc perimeter
  + optional triangle-strip outline ring, matching GLRenderer output
- Cleanup of all three pipelines + layout in cleanupVulkan()

Fix playback race at engine level with load-generation counter

Add loadGen/consumedGen counters to the C++ engine so stale
playback_stop IPC calls (from fire-and-forget stops) cannot clear
sessionPath between a newer playback_load and start_output_stream.

- playbackLoad: ++loadGen before setting sessionPath
- startOutputStreamLocked: consumedGen = loadGen when start_playback
- playbackStopLocked: only clear sessionPath when loadGen == consumedGen

This eliminates the race without serializing stop/start IPC calls,
removing the ~200-500ms latency the JS-level drain introduced.

Revert JS drain: remove _pendingEngineStop await from
enginePlaybackStart and restore void fire-and-forget at all 3
stop call sites (video.js, audio.js×2).
